name: Software
tagline: We design, build and ship the tools we want to use ourselves.
about: Software is the open-source surface of Hushkey. Everything we publish here started as something we needed to ship our own products — frameworks, queues, tools. Once a piece earns its keep internally, we extract it, polish the seams, and give it back.
Why give it back? Because we didn't build any of this from scratch. Deno, Fresh, Preact, Redis, the entire web stack we sit on — every line of it was someone else's gift. Software is the receipt.

Our rule is simple: nothing ships here until it has paid its way inside Hushkey first. That keeps the surface honest. No vapourware, no abandoned scaffolds, no library written in anticipation of a use case that never arrived. If it's on this page, it's running in production today.

Hushkey itself is proprietary — it's the platform that pays for the open-source work. The two halves are intentional: a single commercial bet funds the public toolkit, and the public toolkit forces the commercial product to be honest about its dependencies.

team.name: Leo Termine
team.bio: Solo founder for now — designing, building and shipping every product on this page. Background across web, mobile and infra. Based in Perth.
